judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports nail expectations a–c, but Report 1 additionally flags the missing env-level/global gate (expectation d), making it the stronger answer.

report_2.evidence: When the key is absent from `tenant.settings` (line 11, `.get(...) is None`), the function returns `True` (line 12).

report_1.evidence: I would change line 11-12 in `backend/app/feature_flags.py` (the `auto_send_reminders_enabled` function) so the `is None` branch returns `False` instead of `True`

report_2.evidence: I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` to return `False` when unset

report_1.evidence: `compact_calendar_view` is a pure UI preference (low blast radius, safe to default on), while `auto_send_reminders` triggers real outbound communication to patients.

report_2.evidence: the calendar-view helper was copy-pasted as a template for the new reminders flag... `compact_calendar_view` is a cosmetic UI-only preference where defaulting to "on" is inconsequential... whereas `auto_send_reminders` controls sending real messages to patients.

report_1.evidence: Check whether there's any env-level master switch gating this batch job globally (I did not find one in `backend/app/jobs/reminders.py` or `feature_flags.py`) — ... a per-tenant flag driving outbound messaging should typically be ANDed with an env-level master gate for a controlled rollout, not run unconditionally for every tenant
